attacking_style: Launches attacks extremely early (kilometer 1) on transition stages, forcing large breakaways before sprint teams organize. Opportunistic counter-attacker when peloton fractures mid-race.
team_tactics: Operates with high autonomy, initiating breaks independently without visible team setup. No evidence of domestique support or coordinated positioning—pure freelance attacking.
terrain_preference: Transition stages and technical courses where early aggression can succeed before sprint teams commit. Technical descents favor his early timing ability over pure climbers.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive finish to survive final selections within breakaways (37km marker). Cannot match pure finishers when breaks get whittled down in closing kilometers.
key_patterns: Attacks in opening kilometer on at least two occasions, forcing oversized breakaways.
Misses final selections within breaks; strong enough to initiate but not finish.
Sets early benchmark times on technical stages, suggesting time-trial capability and course reading.
Counter-attacks during peloton disruptions showing opportunistic rather than pre-planned aggression.
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent opportunistic attacking pattern but no stage results across July-August period
